messages.content:
You are a precision classifier trained to tag Skool community replies exactly as Scott Northwolf вҖ” founder of Self-Improvement Nation and Answer 42 вҖ” would tag them himself.

Scott is a high-ticket sales expert who helps self-improvement coaches go from $0 to $10K/month in 42 days. His communication style is raw, brotherhood energy вҖ” direct, high-energy, zero corporate polish. He uses philosophy, ancient wisdom, and self-improvement references naturally.

Your job is to read a post/comment thread and classify the reply at the bottom using three dimensions:

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 1 вҖ” TONE TAGS (multi-select, 1вҖ“4 max)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.
2. Short acknowledgements (emojis, "fire", single words) вҶ’ intent: acknowledgement, tone: [brotherhood] or [hype], stage: nurture.
3. When Scott teases future content or drops mystery вҶ’ always include mystery-teasing or teasing-future-value in tone.
4. Brotherhood tone is almost always present unless the reply is purely informational.
5. Sales stage "ask" is rare вҖ” only when the person has explicitly said they want to buy, book, or inquire about the program.
6. Never pick more than 4 tone tags. Less is more вҖ” only tag what is clearly there.
7. Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No explanation. No markdown. No commentary.

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict JSON, no other text):
{"tone_tags": ["tag1", "tag2"], "intent": "intent-name", "sales_stage": "stage-name"}

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 1 вҖ” TONE TAGS (multi-select, 1вҖ“4 max)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.
2. Short acknowledgements (emojis, "fire", single words) вҶ’ intent: acknowledgement, tone: [brotherhood] or [hype], stage: nurture.
3. When Scott teases future content or drops mystery вҶ’ always include mystery-teasing or teasing-future-value in tone.
4. Brotherhood tone is almost always present unless the reply is purely informational.
5. Sales stage "ask" is rare вҖ” only when the person has explicitly said they want to buy, book, or inquire about the program.
6. Never pick more than 4 tone tags. Less is more вҖ” only tag what is clearly there.
7. Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No explanation. No markdown. No commentary.

вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

[Miguel Ariza Lora]: What's up gang,My name is Miguel, 25 years old and Spanish-Dutch currently living in Thailand.My journey in self-improvement began around 15 years old when I was overweight. I really wanted to be more like the others my age. Luckily this is when YouTube began to take off and people starting sharing a lot of value around fitness and nutrition. Trial and error got me to losing 20kg's eventually, and I've been able to keep most of it off ever since. When I saw putting some work in could give me results, I realized the same thing could be done in other aspects.The following years I became a lot more extroverted, social and confident. Through studying social work and psychology I got into coaching people to build a better life. I was good doing those jobs, and now I've taken it online and moved to Thailand. Felt like a good next step in my self-improvement. Have been coaching people now for 7 years already. Learned a whole lot. About people, training techniques, but probably even more about myself.I just think the self-improvement journey is amazing, and I wish everyone can experience seeing themselves improve into a better version and better life, step by step. Does it ever end? I don't think so. There's always room for improvement. It can feel tiring at times, but I wouldn't want to have it any other way.Can't wait to connect more with all of you guys.- Miguel

--- POST ---
Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: Mindset рҹ§ 
Title: Don't try to escape рҹҳөвҖҚрҹ’«

If you are trying to escape the present moment you are failing in real time.  The masculine error lies on believing that things will be fundamentally different some day.  "Someday my women will stop complaining."  "Someday I'll make enough money and I'll finally be free."  "Someday I'll land a client and everything will change forever."  Guess what?  When you land a client...  You still got to keep doing outreach.  You still got to keep building upwards.  I know because it happened to me.  Don't make the mistake of trying to escape your current situation.  If you stay on this path for long enough you WILL become rich and you WILL miss the days in which you were struggling and striving to hit these goals.  Enjoy the process now.  I know it sounds like a cliche but if you do that and let go of all atatchment to the outcome the inputs will become effortless and everything will flow without frustration.  Delusion backed up just by wo

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

--- POST ---
Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: Mindset рҹ§ 
Title: Don't try to escape рҹҳөвҖҚрҹ’«

If you are trying to escape the present moment you are failing in real time.  The masculine error lies on believing that things will be fundamentally different some day.  "Someday my women will stop complaining."  "Someday I'll make enough money and I'll finally be free."  "Someday I'll land a client and everything will change forever."  Guess what?  When you land a client...  You still got to keep doing outreach.  You still got to keep building upwards.  I know because it happened to me.  Don't make the mistake of trying to escape your current situation.  If you stay on this path for long enough you WILL become rich and you WILL miss the days in which you were struggling and striving to hit these goals.  Enjoy the process now.  I know it sounds like a cliche but if you do that and let go of all atatchment to the outcome the inputs will become effortless and everything will flow without frustration.  Delusion backed up just by wo

Author: Andrew Brooks
Category: General рҹ’ӯ
Title: Food quality in restaurants: probably not for you

How often do you go out, eat somewhere, and think to yourself "well, that wasn't great." And how did you feel afterwards?  There are reasons for the generally low quality of food in most restaurants. Mostly they are limited by their food budget. And the food budget limits the quality they are able to purchase because we also generally want cheap food. What that means is that most of the produce will be conventional produce, not organic. The meats (a large part of the budget) will be of lower quality and the rest will be medium quality at best.  The things we WANT: organic, fresh, healthy, and delicious, simply are not available within the cheap food framework. Unfortunately to get the good stuff you have to pay more. That's just the way it is for now...31Last comment Feb 24

Author: Arthur Maddocks-Brown
Category: The Hero's Journey рҹ—әпёҸ
Title: Introducing Myself!!

Hey Guys!  I am on my own hero's journey!  I am a writer, so I will keep it short & upload a video to help you meet me better!  1. Aged 15 diagnosed with depression, lived the next 15 years with depression and suicide ideation, multiple times a day I would have consistent and persistent thoughts.  2. In 2019 I got engaged to my partner of 7 years (aged 27)  3. We got a dog (2019) 4. COVID happened (2020)  5. We bought some land (2020) 6. October 2020 I was hit by a car   7. We built a house, got married, moved cities and changed jobs (2021)  8. 2021 cracks started forming in the relationship  9. 2022 Christmas I broke down about my mental heath challenges, my physical rehab journey and...  10. 2023 (January) she told me she was leaving...  11. I nearly took my life... pills left over from my car accident in 2020... but my dog walked in the room and I said fuck... I can't do that to you.  12. 2023 the house was sol

Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: The Hero's Journey рҹ—әпёҸ
Title: Not a community but...рҹӣЎпёҸ Brotherhood!!!

I don't know about you but my aims are not to meet people randomly and never see them again even when we actually connected very deeply.  Consistency is not only applicable to your work, your self-improvement and to making progress on those areas of your life.  I believe consistency must be applied to one of the areas that matters the most in our lives, especially as men which is building a strong network of men whom we can ACTUALLY call brothers, and even though I do not believe only with calls and cool conversations something like that can be achieved... it's a start to build trust and get to know each other, see if our values align and if we can actually work together to build something greater than ourselves because THAT'S where true brotherhood is created:  Men with shared values struggling and striving together to achieve a common goal.  So I'll be DMing you guys perdiodically to jump on one on

вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

[Scott Northwolf]: Welcome to the gang, Bruno. Glad to have you with us, brother!One of the biggest things for me was when I stopped thinking about what I wanted and started thinking in terms of what other people wanted and needed.Choosing a niche, going to them and actually asking them consistently and in a methodical way what were their dreams and hopes and what were their biggest problems and using that to build a truly attractive offer that they'd resonated with and they actually wanted to buy and then coming back and offering that to them.And if you picked right the niche and you were able to find them to conduct niche research interviews with them... you'll be able to find them again on the same place and get them on sales' calls with you.Then all that remains is to conduct appointment setting, sales' calls, build a good script and funnel structure and track your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) in order to improve over time, stay consistent and don't switch niches or business models all together because that resets your progress back to 0.

вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)

вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.

DIMENSION 1 вҖ” TONE TAGS (multi-select, 1вҖ“4 max)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.

вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.

  [Scott Northwolf]: @Aeon Bancuyo alright! So, let's break it down:You are afraid of charging high ticket.Why is that?Is it because you feel like your services are no valuable enough?Then tell me...How much do you feel comfortable with charging (no number is wrong, even if it's 0 at the moment)?Now...whatever that number is...Think...how can you make that number 10 times higher?What kind of MASSIVE value could you provide in order to make it worth that amount of money for people?That's a first step towards reframing this limiting belief.It's a basic framework but it will give you an idea of how much you can improve in order to be able to charge the kind of prices that would not just change your life but your clients' lives as well, because the higher ticket they pay the higher the percieved value they'll see in your offer, the higher their commitment and the higher their likelihood of achieving their desired outcome.The second question is a two part thing.First comes clarity in what you offer and then comes the confidence to state the value behind it.Without that clarity is very hard to express confidently how much are you charging for it, because it's hard to see the value when things are chaotic and confusing and confidence is nothing but a proxy for competence.So we start by actually having a VERY well defined outcome (that aligns with the percieved pain/desired of your prospects) enticing enough for the people in your niche (of course, there are certain foundations to this like picking properly the niche, conducting niche research interviews to actually understand in depth the percieved pain your potential clients have and their desired outcome, but I'll be talking in more depth about the roadmap to $10K per month that we follow here on self-improvement Nation soon to make all of this more clear, I've been asked already a lot for this and it's going to be very useful to our members who haven't spoke to me frequently).With regards to the third one.The fact that you are afraid of paying for mentorship is not really about mentors out there (even though obviusly like in all market there are good ones and shittier ones) but it speaks about how you view yourself and ties back to the fear of having people paying you for your services because you don't believe yet your services are that valuable or perhaps that you can deliever effectively, and if you don't believe (yet) in your own coaching... how can you believe in someone else's?This is just the beginning but I hope it does offers you some clarity and makes you reflect in depth about this subject.I'd recommend deep journaling about it and if you have any questions feel free to reach out, Aeon.Like I said, there's a lot more but take it step by step. Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: The Hero's Journey рҹ—әпёҸ
Title: Are you restlessвқ“

Radical change doesn't happen in a vacuum.  Radical change only happens when the pain of staying the same outweights the pain of change.  There's a certain kind of restlessness that is required for such change to take place. Sleepless nights, doubts, fears... frustration? Meeting with reality and having to accept the world is simply NOT how you thought it was. So what will you do now? Take it as insight and modify your behavoir to actually get what you want or stay down, in regret, failure...  People think Self-Improvement is a walk of roses, all about positive mentality and some soft meditation but this is a FUCKING WAR TO THE DEATH with your old self.  There's no pleasure without pain and too much pleasure in the end will bring more pain than you could ever had imagine.  What do you really want out of this life?  Have you ever stopped to ask that to yourself?  And if so...  Have you ever wondered why?  I'll be reading VE

Author: Paul Hedron
Category: General рҹ’ӯ
Title: Basic Leadership Primer

The basic components of a team are as follows:  A group of individuals, a vision, and a facilitator to direct effort and attention.  A leader must be an expert in the nature of the vision, the individuals on the team, and the conditions in which the vision and team interact.  Without a vision or goal, the collection of individuals cannot coalesce. Without individuals to coalesce, a vision or goal is without potential and impetus. Without understanding of the conditions, a team will experience unnecessary inertia, spending effort to achieve the vision.  A vision is merely a blueprint, something to build. The individuals on the team are the builders. The leader is the architect and facilitator.  The base component of a team and shared vision is the individual.  By understanding the individual, a leader has better command of complexity.  An individual has a capable skillset, and limitations. Understanding both the capability/capacity,

[Scott Northwolf]: I'm not really social with people who are not into self-improvement to be honest. The beauty about Bali is that EVERYONE (or at least most people) are into self-improvement so that makes it perfect but I know this is a very rare place, that's also the reason I moved here.Back in the day, when I was still back home I had a big group of friends but when I got into self-improvement I just stopped having things in common with them, our interests just weren't the same and even though I tried to get them on self-improvement I failed. You cannot force people to improve their lives if they don't want to.So basically I endured the time in solitude and used it to not be afraid of being alone and carve my own path, I also used it as motivation to make a shit ton of money and start traveling the world in order to find my tribe which I successfully did here in Bali.On self-improvement you'll go through this phase of loneliness in which you are not high status enough yet to be friends with the people you want to be friends in real life because they are still way above you but if you use that as motivation to get to their level you will get there, it's just a matter of time and commitment.I know it's hard but it's worth it and there's no going back once you take the red pill anyways.The only option is to keep moving forward, brother. Use this community to remind yourself of the people you'll have around you when you make it to the other side and don't lose faith.A lot of us are living here already and we have fulfilled the dream we were shooting for... now we are building together towards a higher aim and I can tell you there's nothing better than making your dreams come true with effort, sacrifice and dedication.

[Andrew Brooks]: I'm not really "social" either. I have about 3 good friends in my locale, and I have others that I connect with virtually every week. But I do feel the need to be social often.So, I invite friends over for dinner, plant to meet up for a coffee or a movie, or just meet up to hang out somewhere for an hour. It doesn't have to be a big thing.But I do plan fun things intentionally. I keep track of the artsy/cultural things happening in my area and will often just show up to some of those and see if I can chat with a new person there. And I will sometimes invite people I meet to connect with me at the event. Sometimes they show, sometimes not. But I'm ok doing things by myself too.So don't feel weird. You aren't. Especially as we get older, the number of friends typically dwindles, but the QUALITY of the friendships goes up. And that's ok.

DIMENSION 1 вҖ” TONE TAGS (multi-select, 1вҖ“4 max)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: Holistic Self-Improvement рҹ“ҡ
Title: Lower the bar to success

Like I always say, to start building momentum and get those early wins that will build up that positive reinforcement early on all you've got to do is to lower the bar to success low enough so you can hit it consistently every day and start leaning just beyond your edge in order to build slowly but surely an unshakable discipline with the good habits.  I just saw a wonderful video by one of our members ( @Laurits Valentin Offersen ) and was delighted to see this poweful mindset at play.  Thank you for making it and sharing it with the world, brother.  Here's the link in case you want to watch it as well: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IVkIO6ECiTM69Last comment Jan 28

вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.

вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.

вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

Title: Be full of yourself, be delusional.

IвҖҷm 18 years old and IвҖҷm fully aware that, on paper, IвҖҷm not that impressive yet.  I donвҖҷt have crazy money.IвҖҷm not a finished product.IвҖҷm still early.  But hereвҖҷs the part most people wonвҖҷt admit:  IвҖҷm delusional about where IвҖҷm going.  Not in an ego way, in a commitment way.I know IвҖҷm getting there because IвҖҷve already decided I wonвҖҷt stop.  That belief is what gets me up at 5 am  That belief is what keeps me working when no oneвҖҷs watching.  That belief is what makes quitting impossible.  People call it arrogance.  I call it certainty.  If I didnвҖҷt believe that strongly in my future self, IвҖҷd move like everyone else, procrastinating, Hesitating and Half-committed.  But I donвҖҷt need proof yet.  I just need to stay consistent.  Being вҖңdelusionalвҖқ is only a problem when you donвҖҷt work.When you do the work, it becomes inevitable.  if you do average ammounts of work you become average  if you do exceptional

Title: Without a timetable you are wasting your life рҹ’Җ

1-Without a timetable you are like a captain on a ship trying to get to his destination with no map or compass. Instant gratification will always be your place of choice and productivity, achievements and actual fulfillment will evade you forever. Thinking from first principles is one of the most powerful things you can do, setting your goal through a process of deep journaling and profound inquiry is the thing that will bring the most clarity in your life. So... how do we go about setting ourselves a proper goal? First we ask to ourselves "What do I want out of life?" Chances are, if you are on this community you want to make money online. вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.

вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.

вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.
2. Short acknowledgements (emojis, "fire", single words) вҶ’ intent: acknowledgement, tone: [brotherhood] or [hype], stage: nurture.
3. When Scott teases future content or drops mystery вҶ’ always include mystery-teasing or teasing-future-value in tone.
4. Brotherhood tone is almost always present unless the reply is purely informational.
5. Sales stage "ask" is rare вҖ” only when the person has explicitly said they want to buy, book, or inquire about the program.
6. Never pick more than 4 tone tags. Less is more вҖ” only tag what is clearly there.
7. Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No explanation. No markdown. No commentary.

[Scott Northwolf]: 3 things:1-Goal setting, drive vacuums identification and reverse engineering of your goal into actionable steps.2-After identifying your goal get inspiration and information about it (e.g. if you want to become a millionaire read the biographies of Andrew Carnegie and Rockefeller, if you want to become a military genious read Alexander's Campagins by Arian and Napoleon: A life).3-Identify your current beliefs with regards to the components of reality that you are struggling with and flip those beliefs in their head, build then a Self-Transcendence Document or Alchemy of Self Workbook.If you want me to explain in more depth those things just shoot me a DM. I know the third one is the "weirdest" one but it requires a LOT of explanation so it makes sense...The logical chain is like this:Experiences form beliefs.Beliefs form your identity.Your identity informs your paradigm (the lenses through which you see the world and interpret it).Your paradigm will inform your percieved options.Your percieved options will inform your actions (you can only make a decision out of the percieved options you believe are available to you).Your actions will solidify (only temporarily) into your behaviour (habits, routines [intentional or unintentional]).Your behaviour will determine your results.

вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.

Author: Lea Newkirk
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: рҹҢ’Synthesis Requires Silence.

We often think of rest as a "reward" for being productive (most times I still do). I am starting to realize thatвҖҷs just bad math. In the high-intensity environment of self-improvement, where we are constantly upgrading our internal software, if I donвҖҷt schedule the off time, my nervous system just schedules a burnout for me. Rest isn't the opposite of productivity, itвҖҷs the essential cooldown phase that allows the next sprint to happen. I mean, we wouldn't run a high-end processor at 100% capacity 24/7, it would melt. Our biology is no different. In the high-intensity environment of self-improvement, where we are constantly upgrading our internal software, if I donвҖҷt schedule the off time, my nervous system just schedules a burnout for me. Our biology is no different. Title: рҹҢ’Synthesis Requires Silence.

We often think of rest as a "reward" for being productive (most times I still do). I am starting to realize thatвҖҷs just bad math. In the high-intensity environment of self-improvement, where we are constantly upgrading our internal software, if I donвҖҷt schedule the off time, my nervous system just schedules a burnout for me. Rest isn't the opposite of productivity, itвҖҷs the essential cooldown phase that allows the next sprint to happen. I mean, we wouldn't run a high-end processor at 100% capacity 24/7, it would melt. Our biology is no different. IвҖҷm curious, in an environment like this, how do you personally distinguish between "healthy friction" and a nervous system thatвҖҷs starting to redline, and what are the specific warning signs you usually miss until itвҖҷs too late?

DIMENSION 1 вҖ” TONE TAGS (multi-select, 1вҖ“4 max)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.
2. Short acknowledgements (emojis, "fire", single words) вҶ’ intent: acknowledgement, tone: [brotherhood] or [hype], stage: nurture.
3. When Scott teases future content or drops mystery вҶ’ always include mystery-teasing or teasing-future-value in tone.
4. Brotherhood tone is almost always present unless the reply is purely informational.
5. Sales stage "ask" is rare вҖ” only when the person has explicitly said they want to buy, book, or inquire about the program.
6. Never pick more than 4 tone tags. Less is more вҖ” only tag what is clearly there.
7. Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No explanation. No markdown. No commentary.

Author: Cris Morales

Title: Networking Fatigue?

I'm all in the strategy of comments, chats, posts, dms, etc. But I do sometimes go to make my daily contributions in a group, and end up leaving a "congrats man!". Sometimes, there is nothing else to say.  Sometimes, groups aren't super active, or they aren't talking about what I know I can help them with. My question to myself and to you guys is  If there is nothing to say, should we even say it? Or should we ALWAYS be making ourselves visible, chatting, commenting, posting in the group, even though it doesn't have much to do with your niche, offer, solution, etc?

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.

вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.

Author: Cris Morales
Category: рҹӨқNetworking
Title: Networking Fatigue?

I'm all in the strategy of comments, chats, posts, dms, etc. But I do sometimes go to make my daily contributions in a group, and end up leaving a "congrats man!". Sometimes, there is nothing else to say.  Sometimes, groups aren't super active, or they aren't talking about what I know I can help them with. My question to myself and to you guys is  If there is nothing to say, should we even say it? --- THREAD ---
[Scott Northwolf]: Less is more.You cannot pour from an empty cup.Fill up your cup.Go read. Learn something new. Meditate. Workout. Stay in silence for a day or two and stop doing outreach (I know it sounds counterproductive but it's what has worked for me).Then you go back only when you have something to say, after a day or two you will...And then magic will happen.Remember that you don't have to do this... you GET to do this! And that's a beautiful priviledge that we have. If we are not having fun and enjoying it, and feeling exactly how we would feel on a 9 to 5 or something like that... then what's the point?Like Nietzsche said "A man can bear almost any HOW as long as he has a WHY."You must see the point in this, otherwise you'll burnout.But sometimes pushing to our limits and actually trying hard (especially to build discipline with regards to outreach and networking) will give you also the beautiful opportunity to experience that sweet contrast between the two approaches and as usual... the most effective way lies somewhere in the middle, when you strike that perfect balance and fluctuate between the two.Life is rhythmic and trying to force reality into one stale structure will always result in dissapointment.Detatch yourself from expectations and learn to enjoy the journey.I know it sounds like a cliche but it has been said by so many wise men for a reason.It is true.I hope this helps, brother.
  [Cris Morales]: @Scott Northwolf Amazing comment man thank you so much for taking the time to write this out!Yes, I understand. Sometimes, going and doing stuff to be able to talk about it is what is going to make networking, commenting, and coming up with content easy! I also understand what you mean by letting that "white noise" come into play, so I can let my brain rest and recharge with new ideasThanks again!

Title: What is your ideal "deep work archetype"?

We have 4 main deep work archetypes.  These are like the 4 classes you can pick inside of a video game. Each have their own benefits, buffs and weaknesses.  They are: - Monastic: 1 main task that you work on for most of the day, minimal breaks. - Bidomal: Long deep work days, but also complete rest days inside the week.  - Rhythmic: Deep work sessions in the morning, shallow work in the afternoon -> evening.  - Journalistic: No clear structure, one minute you could be partying in Bali, the next you are recording          a YT video. Your job is to read a post/comment thread and classify the reply at the bottom using three dimensions:

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 1 вҖ” TONE TAGS (multi-select, 1вҖ“4 max)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

Author: Lea Newkirk
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: The "Learning" Loop рҹ”„

For a long time, I was addicted to the feeling of potential.
IвҖҷd spend hours consuming manifestation videos and different types of self-improvement techniques, convinced I was making progress because I understood the concepts. But my life wasn't actually moving. My mind was racing ahead while my physical reality was standing still. I realized I was using "learning" as a safety blanket. It was a way to feel productive without ever having to risk the discomfort of actually putting myself out there. I was feeding my brain, but starving my results. I found that I didnвҖҷt actually need more videos or books. I needed to take the information out of your head and move it into my daily life.
IвҖҷm curious about your experience:
Have you ever used "researching" as a way to avoid the actual work?
WhatвҖҷs one action youвҖҷre taking today that doesn't involve a screen?
Would love to see where this discussion goes вңЁ

  [Ryan Grant]: (edited)
@Lea Newkirk Something I learnt along the way for sure.
When I first started to explore self improvement I genuinely thought if I was doing my meditations, gratitude journaling etc... everyday I would become successful рҹӨЈ

I really needed this reminder today so thank you! So what is your action for the day?рҹҘ№

  [Ryan Grant]: @Lea Newkirk haha thanks! It's part of the journey I guess. When I realised that meditation etc... are indirect tools that will help me do the things I need to do better, that's when I started to see glimpses of progress :)

Author: Kasparas Stancikas
Category: рҹҚҶFun
Title: рҹҘҠ First Ever Kickboxing Fight... (Knockout, Faked Documents, Work Ethic)

Just before i tell you about the craziest 30 days of my life, i want to thank every single one of you for keeping me accountable under my daily accountability posts:)))
Thanks Synthesizers рҹӨқ
THE STORY:
34 days ago my great friend told me:
"Kasparas, in a month thereвҖҷs this amateur kickboxing competition."
2 seconds later, I said NO because right now i am:
Following synthesizer roadmap and thats my nr1 priority in life
i want to put all my focus into my business
Its too early IвҖҷve only been training for 1 year
Excuses...
But as I continued with my day, this thought stuck in my head. At the end of the day i always had a dream to compete in atleast 1 martial arts fight
My gut intuition was getting stronger and stronger...
So I DMed my trainer to ask if I was even qualified to compete.
I got a response: вҖңOnly if you promise to train really damn hard.вҖқ
And thatвҖҷs where it all started...
I quit bodybuilding and went all in on martial arts training 1-2 times a day and keeping myself accountable in synthesizer every single day for 30 days straight sharing how i am prepping for my fight + scaling my business all at the same time
There was one issue tho...
I still had no clue how would I get my parents to sign the agreement letting me fight? (i am 17yo)
My mom is really against fighting, she HATES it. My dad kind of does too. So, two weeks into training, when it was time to sign the papers, I did what any normal guy would do...
I faked the documents:D I forged my dadвҖҷs signature and everything.
My trainer had no clue, and it all worked out.
So 30 days later, after an insane amount of training and dieting (and still horrible cardio), I was about to step into the ring with another 81kg guy who was a year older and had 3 years of training to rip my head off
Round 1: Went really damn well. I was physically stronger, so I scared him away with some jabs and leg kicks.
Round 2: Still going decent, but I started getting extremely gassed out at the end.
Round 3: I had zero cardio left, luckily my opponent was hella gassed out too. This round was just to see who could survive the longest. And guess what? With only 10 seconds left, I landed a clear hook and KNOCKED HIM OUT, winning the god damn fight!
I still remember my friends screaming in the corner. (Watch the vid below.)
That was one of the BEST moments of my life...
I grew so much as a person, gained so much wisdom, and even managet to CLOSE MULTIPLE HIGH TICKET deals during the prep.
I proved to myself that ANYTHING is possible. If you really want it bad enough you can scale your business and chase your other wild dreams all at the same time
No excuses are valid.
I had to hide from my family, prepare for exams and run a business all at the same time...
if i can do it you can too
What is that dream you been pushing for later? (tell me in the comments)

вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.

вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.

вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
