вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

--- POST ---
Author: Lukas Schmidt
Category: рҹӨ‘ Monetization
Title: I lost the Skool Games. Don't repeat my mistakes

Since I joined Skool back in 2024, I wanted to win the Skool Games.
It is the closest thing to the business Olympics there is.
But that also means you compete against the best.
The best have a massive audience with teams and systems in place to scale a low-ticket offer.
(In the Skool Games, you can charge a maximum of $100 per month or $1200 per year)
I worked a lot more in 26 Q1 compared to 25 Q4, but made a lot less.
Still grateful for the experience and had a lot of fun.
But I wouldn't recommend it to anyone who is getting started.
In the beginning, it is much more effective to charge high ticket and scale as much as possible.
Instead of chasing the shiny trophy, I am committed to building a solid foundation.
I also found it to be much easier to help someone else win.
Congrats again to @Michael Rizk and @Jenna Kelly.
You guys crushed it.
I am also excited to be more active here again, since I am not locked into the competition.
Let's change the world together.
What are you focused on in Q2?

вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.

Author: Piotr Rzepecki
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: Why Most People Never Make Real Money Online (And What Actually Changes Everything)

Most people focus on the wrong things вҶ’ content, followers, looking professional.
But none of that guarantees money.
Attention вү  income. System = income.
Right now, most people operate like this:
Post вҶ’ get views вҶ’ maybe engagement вҶ’ no sales
And then they think the problem is reach, algorithm, or niche.
ItвҖҷs not.
The problem is thereвҖҷs no structure behind what theyвҖҷre doing. No path. No sequence. No flow.
Just random actions that donвҖҷt connect.
HereвҖҷs the shift you need to understand: Most money is NOT made on the first sale
Beginners think: I need one perfect product, I need one perfect offer
Reality is different.
First sale = entry point
Some people even go: Break even or lose money on the first offer вҶ’ just to get the customer in
Why would anyone do that?
Because of this:
1 purchase вҶ’ much higher chance of 2nd purchase
And thatвҖҷs where the real money starts.
Not from convincing strangers once. But from serving people who already trust you.
So instead of thinking: How do I sell this one thing?
You need to think: How do I build a path someone can follow?
A simple structure looks like this:
Attention вҶ’ content, posts, videos
Capture вҶ’ something valuable in exchange for contact
Trust вҶ’ consistency, follow-up, useful content
Offer вҶ’ clear, relevant solution
More value вҶ’ upsells, deeper or faster outcomes
ThatвҖҷs the system.
Another important reality:
~7 interactions вҶ’ before someone buys
So if you post once and expect results вҶ’ wrong expectationIf you post a few times and quit вҶ’ you never even gave it a chance
People need to see you multiple times. They need to understand you. They need to trust you.
Trust is built through repetition.
ThatвҖҷs why follow-up matters so much.
Not aggressive selling. Not spamming links.
But showing up consistently, giving value, explaining things clearly, and slowly removing doubts.
Now hereвҖҷs where most people lose again:
They finally get one sale вҶ’ and stop there
ThatвҖҷs one of the biggest mistakes.
Because:
Best moment to sell again = right after someone buys
At that moment, trust is highest Resistance is lowest.
ThatвҖҷs why this structure works:
Main offer вҶ’ upsell вҶ’ downsell вҶ’ additional value
Important rule:
Main product must already deliver value on its own.
Upsells are not there to fix it. They are there to enhance it.
Example:
Basic solution вҶ’ advanced version Slow result вҶ’ faster result Simple info вҶ’ deeper system
All optional, all relevant.
Now what if someone says no?
Upsell X вҶ’ Downsell
Lower price, Simpler version, Less friction
And many people who said no at first вҶ’ say yes here
Because it feels like a better deal
This is not guesswork. This is based on how people actually buy.
Now zoom out again.
Everything starts with a simple entry point:
Value вҶ’ attention вҶ’ relationship
You give something useful. You attract the right people. You start building trust.
YouвҖҷre no longer chasing random traffic.
YouвҖҷre building a base:
Audience вҶ’ trust вҶ’ repeat buyers
ThatвҖҷs how long-term money is created.
Not from one post. Not from one product.
But from a system that keeps working.
Another mistake people make:
They build once вҶ’ and never test
They assume everything works.
But small issues break everything:
Broken links, confusing offers, bad flow.
And every mistake = lost money
And you donвҖҷt get those people back
ThatвҖҷs why people who get results:
Test everything. Go through the process themselves. Fix problems early Improve step by step.
Because once traffic starts coming in, You need the system to work.
Now compare both approaches:
No system: Post вҶ’ hope вҶ’ no results
System: Attention вҶ’ Trust вҶ’ Offer вҶ’ More value
One is random. One is controlled.
And control is everything.
Because when you control the system, You control the outcome.
You know whatвҖҷs working. You know whatвҖҷs not. You know what to fix.
And thatвҖҷs when things start to scale.
So if you feel stuck:
ItвҖҷs not your content. ItвҖҷs not your niche. ItвҖҷs not your potential.
ItвҖҷs the lack of structure behind what youвҖҷre doing.
You donвҖҷt need more ideas.
You need a system that turns attention into results.
Build it once. Improve it over time.
And everything changes.

Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.

вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.

вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)

вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.
2. Short acknowledgements (emojis, "fire", single words) вҶ’ intent: acknowledgement, tone: [brotherhood] or [hype], stage: nurture.
3. When Scott teases future content or drops mystery вҶ’ always include mystery-teasing or teasing-future-value in tone.
4. Brotherhood tone is almost always present unless the reply is purely informational.
5. Sales stage "ask" is rare вҖ” only when the person has explicitly said they want to buy, book, or inquire about the program.

--- POST ---
Author: Brandon Maloney
Category: рҹҚҶFun
Title: Do you use Push or Pull Motivation?

I saw this podcast by Alex Hormozi and Tony Robbins, and it's always stuck out to me the concept of Push versus Pull motivation.
(You can watch it here: https://youtu.be/xz-ymFOhBAE?si=9jY06iWkMi3vSZd1)
Push motivation is a reuiqrement, and obligation, a "have to" mindset that forces you to use willpower.
Pull motivation is a desire, a want, a purposeful NEED to do the thing, without require as much, or any willpower.
I saw this post by @Aathraey Shrikanth, asking what we would teach our younger selves, and I wouldn't "teach myself anything", but I would 100% want to see what would happen if I told myself to rely on pull instead of push.

Author: Brandon Maloney
Category: рҹҚҶFun
Title: Do you use Push or Pull Motivation?

I saw this podcast by Alex Hormozi and Tony Robbins, and it's always stuck out to me the concept of Push versus Pull motivation.
(You can watch it here: https://youtu.be/xz-ymFOhBAE?si=9jY06iWkMi3vSZd1)
Push motivation is a reuiqrement, and obligation, a "have to" mindset that forces you to use willpower.
Pull motivation is a desire, a want, a purposeful NEED to do the thing, without require as much, or any willpower.
I saw this post by @Aathraey Shrikanth, asking what we would teach our younger selves, and I wouldn't "teach myself anything", but I would 100% want to see what would happen if I told myself to rely on pull instead of push.
(That post is here: https://www.skool.com/synthesizer/what-lesson-would-you-teach-your-younger-self)
What do you guys think about this, what are you running on, and what's your experience with the opposite kind of motivation (if any).

--- POST ---
Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: What's the soul?

@Michael Pluszek recently made a post that called my attention (https://www.skool.com/synthesizer/soul-vs-identity?p=21a05ba9) and I gave a brief introductory explanation of the structure of reality based on the Hermetic Tradition Model.
I called it part one of my answer and here it is:
For clarity and simplicities' sake I'll omit some details and break it down into a simplistic model developed by the ancient Hermetics.
Reality is structured in different layers of organization.
1-Source consciousness (the void, the one, what ancient Greeks in the Pythagorean tradition used to call the Monad).
2-The Mental Plain (The Duat).
3-The causal plain (where there's an observer, an object and a subject, you need three elements to have an experience, you need an object, a subject and an option, an experience itself).
4-The Astral Plain (what's called the Quantum Field of Possibilities, where ideas, language, feelings and imagination exist as components of reality we can percieve).
5-Physical reality (the manifestation and integration of all the above components).
To understand identity and the soul we need to understand first this structure because it's foundational to the understanding that flows from this. If we don't take this as the basis the rest will not make sense and to explain why this is so it will take... well, a lot of time, so let's just suspend disbelief and accept this framework as a precodition for the following knowledge to unfold itself.
If you want to get the full explanation just let me know and I'll write the second part of this...
///////////// ///////////// ///////////// ///////////// /////////////
Since some of you, guys, requested for the second part here it goes ( @Lea Newkirk @Victor Floden @Michael Pluszek ):
The soul is an unchanging configuration of pure consciousness, an emanation of the Source at the level of the mental and causal plain linking them both together.
Mental Plane вҶ’ Soul вҶ’ Causal Plane вҶ’ Astral вҶ’ Physical
The soul serves the function of incarnating into the physical plain, it has two main paths:
It will either descend into density by entangling itself with matter and the lower insticts generating karma or...
It will release karma through 'gnosis' (knowlege of its divine essence) and return to source through a purification process.
This is not as simple as I'm painting it to be.
Souls on their path out of source and back to it won't follow a linear downwards and then upwards trend during a cycle of reincarnations until EVERYTHING returns to source, it will look more like EVERY single possible combination ascending during some reincarnations, going 'downwards' during others and ascending again and like that on many different ways throughout all the possible reincarnations that can occur during a full cycle.
This cycles can be viewed as a cosmic breath of sorts.
I'd love to answer any questions that you guys might come up in the comments and clarify everything in case I haven't explained it with enough clarity for you.

Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: What's the soul?

@Michael Pluszek recently made a post that called my attention (https://www.skool.com/synthesizer/soul-vs-identity?p=21a05ba9) and I gave a brief introductory explanation of the structure of reality based on the Hermetic Tradition Model.
I called it part one of my answer and here it is:
For clarity and simplicities' sake I'll omit some details and break it down into a simplistic model developed by the ancient Hermetics.
Reality is structured in different layers of organization.
1-Source consciousness (the void, the one, what ancient Greeks in the Pythagorean tradition used to call the Monad).
2-The Mental Plain (The Duat).
3-The causal plain (where there's an observer, an object and a subject, you need three elements to have an experience, you need an object, a subject and an option, an experience itself).
4-The Astral Plain (what's called the Quantum Field of Possibilities, where ideas, language, feelings and imagination exist as components of reality we can percieve).
5-Physical reality (the manifestation and integration of all the above components).
To understand identity and the soul we need to understand first this structure because it's foundational to the understanding that flows from this. If we don't take this as the basis the rest will not make sense and to explain why this is so it will take... well, a lot of time, so let's just suspend disbelief and accept this framework as a precodition for the following knowledge to unfold itself.
If you want to get the full explanation just let me know and I'll write the second part of this...
///////////// ///////////// ///////////// ///////////// /////////////
Since some of you, guys, requested for the second part here it goes ( @Lea Newkirk @Victor Floden @Michael Pluszek ):
The soul is an unchanging configuration of pure consciousness, an emanation of the Source at the level of the mental and causal plain linking them both together.
Mental Plane вҶ’ Soul вҶ’ Causal Plane вҶ’ Astral вҶ’ Physical
The soul serves the function of incarnating into the physical plain, it has two main paths:
It will either descend into density by entangling itself with matter and the lower insticts generating karma or...
It will release karma through 'gnosis' (knowlege of its divine essence) and return to source through a purification process.
This is not as simple as I'm painting it to be.
Souls on their path out of source and back to it won't follow a linear downwards and then upwards trend during a cycle of reincarnations until EVERYTHING returns to source, it will look more like EVERY single possible combination ascending during some reincarnations, going 'downwards' during others and ascending again and like that on many different ways throughout all the possible reincarnations that can occur during a full cycle.
This cycles can be viewed as a cosmic breath of sorts.
I'd love to answer any questions that you guys might come up in the comments and clarify everything in case I haven't explained it with enough clarity for you.

Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: What's the soul?

@Michael Pluszek recently made a post that called my attention (https://www.skool.com/synthesizer/soul-vs-identity?p=21a05ba9) and I gave a brief introductory explanation of the structure of reality based on the Hermetic Tradition Model.
I called it part one of my answer and here it is:
For clarity and simplicities' sake I'll omit some details and break it down into a simplistic model developed by the ancient Hermetics.
Reality is structured in different layers of organization.
1-Source consciousness (the void, the one, what ancient Greeks in the Pythagorean tradition used to call the Monad).
2-The Mental Plain (The Duat).
3-The causal plain (where there's an observer, an object and a subject, you need three elements to have an experience, you need an object, a subject and an option, an experience itself).
4-The Astral Plain (what's called the Quantum Field of Possibilities, where ideas, language, feelings and imagination exist as components of reality we can percieve).
5-Physical reality (the manifestation and integration of all the above components).
To understand identity and the soul we need to understand first this structure because it's foundational to the understanding that flows from this. If we don't take this as the basis the rest will not make sense and to explain why this is so it will take... well, a lot of time, so let's just suspend disbelief and accept this framework as a precodition for the following knowledge to unfold itself.
If you want to get the full explanation just let me know and I'll write the second part of this...
///////////// ///////////// ///////////// ///////////// /////////////
Since some of you, guys, requested for the second part here it goes ( @Lea Newkirk @Victor Floden @Michael Pluszek ):
The soul is an unchanging configuration of pure consciousness, an emanation of the Source at the level of the mental and causal plain linking them both together.
Mental Plane вҶ’ Soul вҶ’ Causal Plane вҶ’ Astral вҶ’ Physical
The soul serves the function of incarnating into the physical plain, it has two main paths:
It will either descend into density by entangling itself with matter and the lower insticts generating karma or...
It will release karma through 'gnosis' (knowlege of its divine essence) and return to source through a purification process.
This is not as simple as I'm painting it to be.
Souls on their path out of source and back to it won't follow a linear downwards and then upwards trend during a cycle of reincarnations until EVERYTHING returns to source, it will look more like EVERY single possible combination ascending during some reincarnations, going 'downwards' during others and ascending again and like that on many different ways throughout all the possible reincarnations that can occur during a full cycle.
This cycles can be viewed as a cosmic breath of sorts.
I'd love to answer any questions that you guys might come up in the comments and clarify everything in case I haven't explained it with enough clarity for you.

DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)

вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.

DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)

вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.

вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.

вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

--- POST ---
Author: Scott Northwolf
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: Don't digress. Be simple...

Recently I was on a coaching call with one of my clients and at a certain point I was going rounds and rounds trying to break one of his limiting beliefs and he just said to me "Brother, be simple. Get to the point."
I could tell how tired he was of me just repeating myself every time we'd disagree and I realized then...
"Wait a minute! Why is this not working? Why is this taking so long? Why do I feel like I'm speaking for ages and getting nowhere?"
Then it struck me even harder when I was on two separate calls with other coaches while we were talking about business.
They'd digress like crazy and it'd feel like pure ADHD every step of the conversation. Then I realized they were a mirror to my own behaviour and I could tell how tiring it was the whole deal.
Since then I have decided to try to keep it simple.
Condensed the biggest amount of high leverage knowledge in the shortest sentences possible and focus on actionable steps way more than on overexplaining super complex concepts just to satisfy my own ego and show just how smart I am (I am, and also the most humble man in the universe in case you haven't been able to tell by now).
Being comfortable speaking less and even remaining in silence it's an art and it brings you more power, not less.
Striking the balance is essential.
Speak too much and you'll come across weak and as if you have something to proof.
We all have something to proof but we must start by proving that to ourselves so it comes off naturally and it's projected from a place of abundance, groundness and relaxation instead of from a place of lack and fear.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remeber: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

You also summed up the conclusion I would put to it : "Striking the balance is essential."
We have a depth and thoughtfulness superpower.
With great power comes great responsibilities, and it is ours to make our wisdom accessible and actionable for those that can (and want to) benefit from it, in the way that hits home for them.

  [Stan Morrish]: @Jeremiah Bergeron Literally, bro

Title: An observation from someone a little further down the life arc

One thing IвҖҷve really enjoyed about spending time in Synthesizer is the energy in the room.
There are a lot of builders here вҖ” people thinking deeply about offers, systems, momentum, and the long game of building something meaningful.
IвҖҷve learned a lot just by listening to the conversations.
At the same time, I sometimes realize IвҖҷm observing the room from a slightly different vantage point.
Most of the people here are younger entrepreneurs building businesses.
Meanwhile, IвҖҷm a little further down the life arc, and my work focuses on helping people build strong, supportive relationships.
So I often find myself quietly watching the conversations about growth, focus, and grinding forward.
From where I sit, something eventually becomes very clear for most people:
At some point, the thing that either supports or destabilizes everything youвҖҷre building isnвҖҷt strategy.
ItвҖҷs your relationships.
Timing plays a big role in when people realize that.
So IвҖҷm curious:
Have you ever noticed that the people who benefit most from what you do often havenвҖҷt reached the moment in their life where they can see it yet?

Title: An observation from someone a little further down the life arc

One thing IвҖҷve really enjoyed about spending time in Synthesizer is the energy in the room.
There are a lot of builders here вҖ” people thinking deeply about offers, systems, momentum, and the long game of building something meaningful.
IвҖҷve learned a lot just by listening to the conversations.
At the same time, I sometimes realize IвҖҷm observing the room from a slightly different vantage point.
Most of the people here are younger entrepreneurs building businesses.
Meanwhile, IвҖҷm a little further down the life arc, and my work focuses on helping people build strong, supportive relationships.

From where I sit, something eventually becomes very clear for most people:
At some point, the thing that either supports or destabilizes everything youвҖҷre building isnвҖҷt strategy.
ItвҖҷs your relationships.
Timing plays a big role in when people realize that.

Have you ever noticed that the people who benefit most from what you do often havenвҖҷt reached the moment in their life where they can see it yet?

Title: do you let your fav posts disappear? рҹ”Ҙ

Sometimes I feel scared of making a banger post to a community if I know that it could fade away into the feed and get forgotten рҹ‘¶
Yet in many ways, I kinda like that.
On Skool, and especially in Synthesizer, posting to the community feels like itвҖҷs based off of the unique energy of the day.
ItвҖҷs less about how polished the writing is, and more about how attuned it is to the community atmosphere at any given moment.

Thought leadership posts fail, honest call-to-action questions are appreciated, and posts that spark conversation work best.
These guidelines help us focus on whatвҖҷs in front of us TODAY and NOW. Not on whatвҖҷs in the future.
But it makes me wonder what we can do with those posts that we're proud of, once they've been swept away into the feed.
Asking u guys...

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Choose only the tones that are genuinely present. Do not force tags.

вҖў hype               вҖ“ Maximum energy, excitement, ALL CAPS on key words. "LETS FUCKIN GOOO". Peak Scott mode.
вҖў brotherhood        вҖ“ Raw male comradery. "brother", "bro", "king". Street-level loyalty, not corporate cheerfulness.
вҖў motivational       вҖ“ Pushing someone forward with conviction. "Only when the pain of staying the same outweighs the pain of change..."
вҖў authority          вҖ“ Scott positioning himself as THE expert. Drops credentials naturally. No arrogance вҖ” just certainty.
вҖў direct             вҖ“ No fluff. Gets straight to the point. Short sentences. Often gives the answer in the first line.
вҖў casual             вҖ“ Low-key, no agenda. Like texting a friend. "lol", "yeah bro". Not trying to impress.
вҖў self-aggrandization вҖ“ Scott referencing his own wins or lifestyle вҖ” creates aspiration, not annoyance.
вҖў teasing-future-value вҖ“ Hinting at something big coming without revealing it. Creates curiosity and FOMO.
вҖў praise             вҖ“ Genuine recognition of effort or insight. Specific, not generic.
вҖў humor              вҖ“ Light jokes or sarcasm. Never mean, always in good spirit.
вҖў empathy            вҖ“ Acknowledging someone's struggle. Brief and real вҖ” he doesn't dwell. Then pivots.
вҖў storytelling       вҖ“ Using a personal story or anecdote to make a point.
вҖў vulnerability      вҖ“ Briefly revealing a personal challenge вҖ” creates trust. Rare and powerful.
вҖў tough-love         вҖ“ Direct honest feedback that might sting but is said with care.
вҖў mystery-teasing    вҖ“ Creating intrigue around Scott's methods or lifestyle. Makes people want to ask more.
вҖў chit-chat          вҖ“ Pure social conversation. No agenda, no value delivery. Just being human.
вҖў bonding-rapport    вҖ“ Building personal connection through shared experiences or references.
вҖў gratitude          вҖ“ Scott expressing genuine thanks. Rare but real.
вҖў curiosity          вҖ“ Scott asking a question because he genuinely wants to know more.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 2 вҖ” INTENT (single, pick the PRIMARY purpose)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў acknowledgement    вҖ“ Simply reacting to what was said. Emoji, "fire", "exactly". No sales agenda вҖ” just being present.
вҖў engagement-nurture вҖ“ Keeping the conversation alive and building warmth. Makes the person feel seen and come back.
вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.
2. Short acknowledgements (emojis, "fire", single words) вҶ’ intent: acknowledgement, tone: [brotherhood] or [hype], stage: nurture.
3. When Scott teases future content or drops mystery вҶ’ always include mystery-teasing or teasing-future-value in tone.
4. Brotherhood tone is almost always present unless the reply is purely informational.
5. Sales stage "ask" is rare вҖ” only when the person has explicitly said they want to buy, book, or inquire about the program.
6. Never pick more than 4 tone tags. Less is more вҖ” only tag what is clearly there.
7. Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No explanation. No markdown. No commentary.

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict JSON, no other text):
{"tone_tags": ["tag1", "tag2"], "intent": "intent-name", "sales_stage": "stage-name"}

--- POST ---
Author: Christy Sanderson
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: When I Stopped Reposting and Started Proving

There was a time when I could articulate powerful ideas.
Clarity. Discipline. Identity. Structure. Consistency.
I could write about them. Teach them. Quote them.
But there was a quiet gap between what I said and what I had actually tested under pressure.
I remember one particular week вҖ” overwhelmed, financially stretched, emotionally tired. I had been speaking about consistency and structure publiclyвҖҰ but privately, my own systems were fragile.
ThatвҖҷs when it hit me.
ItвҖҷs easy to repost wisdom when life is calm.
ItвҖҷs different to live it when life pushes back.
My pain point wasnвҖҷt hypocrisy.
It was realization.
I had absorbed powerful ideas from books, podcasts, communities вҖ” and I believed them. But belief is not embodiment.
Pressure reveals what youвҖҷve actually integrated.
So I made a decision.
I stopped sharing ideas I hadnвҖҷt personally run through fire.
I stopped echoing what sounded impressive.
I started asking:
Does this hold up when IвҖҷm tired?
Does this work when money is tight?
Does this help when doubt creeps in?
Does this produce results consistently?

--- POST ---
Author: Christy Sanderson
Category: рҹ’…Other
Title: When I Stopped Reposting and Started Proving

There was a time when I could articulate powerful ideas.
Clarity. Discipline. Identity. Structure. Consistency.
I could write about them. Teach them. Quote them.
But there was a quiet gap between what I said and what I had actually tested under pressure.
I remember one particular week вҖ” overwhelmed, financially stretched, emotionally tired. I had been speaking about consistency and structure publiclyвҖҰ but privately, my own systems were fragile.
ThatвҖҷs when it hit me.
ItвҖҷs easy to repost wisdom when life is calm.
ItвҖҷs different to live it when life pushes back.
My pain point wasnвҖҷt hypocrisy.
It was realization.
I had absorbed powerful ideas from books, podcasts, communities вҖ” and I believed them. But belief is not embodiment.
Pressure reveals what youвҖҷve actually integrated.
So I made a decision.
I stopped sharing ideas I hadnвҖҷt personally run through fire.
I stopped echoing what sounded impressive.
I started asking:
Does this hold up when IвҖҷm tired?
Does this work when money is tight?
Does this help when doubt creeps in?
Does this produce results consistently?
And something changed.
My voice got quieter вҖ” but stronger.
My content got simpler вҖ” but sharper.
Because now, when I say something works, itвҖҷs not because it sounds right.
ItвҖҷs because IвҖҷve watched it survive stress.
ThereвҖҷs a difference between information and proof.
Reposting spreads ideas.
Pressure-testing builds authority.
And authority built through lived experience canвҖҷt be shaken by trends.
What idea have you personally pressure-tested that most people only repost?

вҖў community-building вҖ“ Reinforcing the identity and culture of Self-Improvement Nation.
вҖў authority-proofing вҖ“ Demonstrating Scott's expertise or results without being asked. Builds credibility passively.
вҖў value-delivery     вҖ“ Giving a specific, actionable insight or framework that is immediately useful.
вҖў close-to-call      вҖ“ Inviting the person to book a call or DM. Only when they've shown clear buying signals.
вҖў social-proof       вҖ“ Highlighting wins or transformations to attract others.
вҖў redirect           вҖ“ Moving the conversation toward Scott's core offer. Smooth, not abrupt.
вҖў info-gathering     вҖ“ Asking a question to learn more about the person's situation or goals.
вҖў lead-qualification вҖ“ Probing to determine if this person is a coach who could become a buyer.
вҖў pain-agitation     вҖ“ Amplifying someone's problem to make the need for a solution more urgent.
вҖў objection-handling вҖ“ Addressing a doubt or pushback and flipping it into a reason to move forward.
вҖў funneling          вҖ“ Directing the person toward Scott's community, program, or resources.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
DIMENSION 3 вҖ” SALES STAGE (single, where is THIS PERSON in their journey?)
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҖў awareness  вҖ“ Person just discovered Scott. Make a good first impression. No selling.
вҖў engagement вҖ“ Person is active but hasn't shown buying intent. Deepen the relationship.
вҖў nurture    вҖ“ Person is warm and trusts Scott. Stay top of mind, deliver value.
вҖў ask        вҖ“ Person has shown clear buying signals. Move them toward a call.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
CLASSIFICATION RULES
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
1. Read the FULL thread context before classifying. The sales stage depends on the PERSON'S behavior, not Scott's reply.
2. Short acknowledgements (emojis, "fire", single words) вҶ’ intent: acknowledgement, tone: [brotherhood] or [hype], stage: nurture.
3. When Scott teases future content or drops mystery вҶ’ always include mystery-teasing or teasing-future-value in tone.
4. Brotherhood tone is almost always present unless the reply is purely informational.
5. Sales stage "ask" is rare вҖ” only when the person has explicitly said they want to buy, book, or inquire about the program.
6. Never pick more than 4 tone tags. Less is more вҖ” only tag what is clearly there.
7. Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No explanation. No markdown. No commentary.

Author: Christy Sanderson

Title: When I Stopped Reposting and Started Proving

There was a time when I could articulate powerful ideas.
Clarity. Discipline. Identity. Structure. Consistency.
I could write about them. Teach them. Quote them.
But there was a quiet gap between what I said and what I had actually tested under pressure.
I remember one particular week вҖ” overwhelmed, financially stretched, emotionally tired. I had been speaking about consistency and structure publiclyвҖҰ but privately, my own systems were fragile.
ThatвҖҷs when it hit me.
ItвҖҷs easy to repost wisdom when life is calm.
ItвҖҷs different to live it when life pushes back.
My pain point wasnвҖҷt hypocrisy.
It was realization.
I had absorbed powerful ideas from books, podcasts, communities вҖ” and I believed them. But belief is not embodiment.
Pressure reveals what youвҖҷve actually integrated.
So I made a decision.
I stopped sharing ideas I hadnвҖҷt personally run through fire.
I stopped echoing what sounded impressive.
I started asking:
Does this hold up when IвҖҷm tired?
Does this work when money is tight?
Does this help when doubt creeps in?
Does this produce results consistently?
And something changed.
My voice got quieter вҖ” but stronger.
My content got simpler вҖ” but sharper.
Because now, when I say something works, itвҖҷs not because it sounds right.
ItвҖҷs because IвҖҷve watched it survive stress.
ThereвҖҷs a difference between information and proof.
Reposting spreads ideas.
Pressure-testing builds authority.
And authority built through lived experience canвҖҷt be shaken by trends.
What idea have you personally pressure-tested that most people only repost?
